chunk_summary: This section provides quick reference anchors for outpatient macronutrient decisions in children, emphasizing growth assessment, macronutrient distribution, hydration, and program-specific guidelines like PM POSHAN meals.
learning_objectives: Understand age-specific macronutrient distribution ranges and hydration targets for children.
Learn how to address growth faltering and obesity in outpatient pediatric care.
Incorporate program-specific guidelines, such as PM POSHAN, into nutritional counseling.
Apply WHO and IAP growth standards to assess and monitor pediatric growth.
key_facts: Macronutrient distribution (AMDR): Fat 30–40% (1–3 years), 25–35% (4–18 years); Carbohydrate 45–65%; Protein 5–20% (1–3 years), 10–30% (4–18 years).
Fibre targets: 19 g/day (1–3 years), 25 g/day (4–8 years), 31 g/day boys and 26 g/day girls (9–13 years), 38 g/day males and 26 g/day females (14–18 years).
Hydration (total water AI): 1.3 L/day (1–3 years), 1.7 L/day (4–8 years), increasing with heat and activity.
Growth faltering: Aim for weight gain 2–3× the median for age; reassess at 2 weeks and escalate at 4 weeks if no improvement.
PM POSHAN meals provide ~450 kcal/12 g protein (primary) and ~700 kcal/20 g protein (upper primary); families should supplement with protein and fruit.
reference_quote: Macronutrient distribution anchor (AMDR): ages 1–3 years fat 30–40% of energy; ages 4–18 years fat 25–35%; carbohydrate 45–65%; protein 5–20% (1–3) and 10–30% (4–18).
document_key_topics: Macronutrient distribution and hydration targets for children
Obesity management in pediatric outpatient care
Integration of PM POSHAN program into nutritional planning
